crops.name: Tomato
crops.summary: A sun-loving fruiting crop suited to roomy containers and garden beds.
crops.watering: Keep evenly moist; avoid wetting leaves late in the day.
instructions: Start seed in a shallow nursery tray.
Move the strongest seedling after 4–6 true leaves.
Stake early and feed after flowering begins.
crops.common_mistake: Using a small pot or watering irregularly, which can split fruit.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Chilli
crops.summary: A productive warm-season crop that grows well in medium containers.
crops.watering: Water when the top layer begins to dry; never leave roots waterlogged.
instructions: Sow thinly in a nursery tray.
Transplant one sturdy seedling per pot.
Pinch the first weak growth to encourage branching.
crops.common_mistake: Overwatering young seedlings and keeping them in low light.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Brinjal
crops.summary: A warm-season container vegetable that rewards steady sun and feeding.
crops.watering: Water deeply when the top 2 cm dries.
instructions: Raise seedlings in a warm nursery tray.
Transplant when plants have 4–6 leaves.
Support fruiting branches and inspect leaf undersides weekly.
crops.common_mistake: Crowding several plants into one container.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Okra
crops.summary: A fast, heat-friendly vegetable for sunny containers and beds.
crops.watering: Water deeply and allow the surface to dry slightly.
instructions: Soak seed for 8–12 hours.
Sow two seeds and retain the strongest seedling.
Pick pods young every two or three days.
crops.common_mistake: Leaving mature pods on the plant, which slows new production.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Cucumber
crops.summary: A quick climbing crop that appreciates warmth, moisture and a sturdy support.
crops.watering: Maintain even moisture during flowering and fruiting.
instructions: Sow two seeds near a support.
Retain one strong seedling.
Guide vines onto the trellis without bending stems.
crops.common_mistake: Allowing the root zone to dry while fruits are developing.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Carrot
crops.summary: A cool-season root crop for deep, loose containers without stones.
crops.watering: Keep the seedbed lightly and consistently moist.
instructions: Use a fine, stone-free growing mix.
Sow shallowly and keep moist until emergence.
Thin early so roots have room to expand.
crops.common_mistake: Transplanting seedlings or using compacted soil, which forks roots.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Radish
crops.summary: One of the quickest root crops for beginners and compact gardens.
crops.watering: Keep moisture steady to prevent woody or split roots.
instructions: Sow directly in loose soil.
Thin seedlings once their first true leaves open.
Harvest promptly at the variety's expected size.
crops.common_mistake: Leaving roots in warm soil too long, which makes them pithy.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Beetroot
crops.summary: A cool-season root vegetable with edible roots and tender leaves.
crops.watering: Maintain even moisture without waterlogging.
instructions: Soak seed clusters briefly before sowing.
Thin clusters to one seedling per position.
Harvest when shoulders reach the expected size.
crops.common_mistake: Failing to thin multi-seed clusters.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Onion
crops.summary: A cool-season bulb crop that needs loose soil and patient, steady growth.
crops.watering: Water lightly and regularly; reduce watering as tops fall.
instructions: Raise seedlings closely in a tray.
Transplant slender seedlings into loose soil.
Stop watering when tops naturally bend and yellow.
crops.common_mistake: Planting too deeply, which restricts bulb expansion.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Spinach
crops.summary: A compact leafy crop for repeated harvests in mild weather.
crops.sunlight: 3–5 hours of sun or bright outdoor light
crops.watering: Keep the shallow root zone consistently moist.
instructions: Sow thinly across a broad container.
Thin crowded patches early.
Harvest outer leaves and leave the centre growing.
crops.common_mistake: Growing in intense heat, which causes quick bolting.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Amaranth
crops.summary: A quick leafy green that tolerates warmth better than many cool-season leaves.
crops.watering: Water lightly but frequently during establishment.
instructions: Mix fine seed with dry sand for even sowing.
Keep the surface moist until emergence.
Cut young leaves above the crown for regrowth.
crops.common_mistake: Burying the tiny seed too deeply.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Cauliflower
crops.summary: A cool-season brassica needing consistent care and enough space for one head.
crops.watering: Keep soil evenly moist throughout head formation.
instructions: Raise seedlings in cool, bright conditions.
Transplant without disturbing the root ball.
Fold outer leaves over a pale head when needed.
crops.common_mistake: Choosing a variety unsuited to the local temperature window.

crops.name: Cabbage
crops.summary: A cool-season leafy head crop for large containers or garden beds.
crops.watering: Maintain steady moisture without saturating the crown.
instructions: Start seed in a nursery tray.
Move stocky seedlings into enriched soil.
Harvest when the head is firm under gentle pressure.
crops.common_mistake: Crowding plants, which prevents firm heads.

crops.name: Garden Peas
crops.summary: A cool-season climber with sweet pods and compact vertical growth.
crops.watering: Keep moist during flowering; avoid soggy soil.
instructions: Sow beside a net or twig support.
Guide young tendrils gently.
Pick pods frequently while they remain tender.
crops.common_mistake: Sowing during warm weather, resulting in weak flowering.

crops.name: French Bean
crops.summary: A productive bean for containers or beds, available in bush and climbing forms.
crops.watering: Water evenly, especially as flowers and pods form.
instructions: Sow directly into moist, draining soil.
Provide support for climbing varieties.
Harvest slender pods frequently.
crops.common_mistake: Handling or harvesting plants while foliage is wet.

crops.name: Bitter Gourd
crops.summary: A vigorous warm-season vine that performs best on a strong trellis.
crops.watering: Water deeply and consistently without flooding roots.
instructions: Nick or soak the hard seed coat overnight.
Sow beside a tall trellis.
Train vines and harvest fruit before it turns yellow.
crops.common_mistake: Giving the vine weak support or too little root space.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Bottle Gourd
crops.summary: A large, fast vine for terraces and gardens with overhead support.
crops.watering: Provide deep, regular watering during vine and fruit growth.
instructions: Sow two seeds in a large final container.
Retain one strong plant and train it upward.
Support hanging fruit and harvest while tender.
crops.common_mistake: Trying to grow the vigorous vine in a small pot.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Pumpkin
crops.summary: A sprawling warm-season vine for a roomy terrace or garden.
crops.watering: Water deeply at the base and mulch to retain moisture.
instructions: Sow in a large mound or container.
Direct vines into open space or onto heavy support.
Allow mature fruit skin to harden before cutting.
crops.common_mistake: Underestimating the space and feeding required by the vine.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Coriander
crops.summary: A quick culinary herb for cool months and shallow containers.
crops.watering: Keep evenly moist but never soggy.
instructions: Gently split the round seed clusters.
Sow directly and cover lightly.
Harvest outer stems before plants bolt.
crops.common_mistake: Transplanting seedlings or sowing in severe heat.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Fenugreek
crops.summary: A forgiving leafy herb that produces a quick cut-and-grow harvest.
crops.watering: Keep the shallow bed moist during germination.
instructions: Scatter seed evenly in a broad container.
Cover lightly and mist until emergence.
Cut above the crown for a second flush.
crops.common_mistake: Sowing too densely without thinning.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Mint
crops.summary: A vigorous perennial herb best kept in its own container.
crops.sunlight: 2–5 hours of morning sun or bright light
crops.watering: Keep moist, especially in warm weather.
instructions: Start with rooted cuttings or a nursery plant when possible.
Keep mint in a separate pot.
Pinch tips frequently for bushy growth.
crops.common_mistake: Planting mint directly beside slower herbs, which it can overwhelm.

source.title: Home and roof garden crop selection

crops.name: Basil
crops.summary: An aromatic warm-season herb for sunny windows, balconies and gardens.
crops.watering: Water when the top centimetre feels dry.
instructions: Surface-sow seed and cover very lightly.
Move seedlings without burying the stem.
Pinch flower buds to extend leaf harvest.
crops.common_mistake: Letting the plant flower early instead of pinching tips.

crops.name: Dill
crops.summary: A delicate cool-season herb that prefers direct sowing and gentle sun.
crops.watering: Keep evenly moist during establishment.
instructions: Sow directly because dill dislikes root disturbance.
Thin seedlings carefully.
Harvest feathery leaves before flowering.
crops.common_mistake: Transplanting established seedlings and damaging their taproots.

crops.name: Parsley
crops.summary: A slow-germinating cool-season herb for bright balconies and windows.
crops.watering: Keep consistently moist while seed germinates.
instructions: Soak seed overnight to encourage germination.
Be patient and keep the surface moist.
Harvest outer stalks from the base.
crops.common_mistake: Discarding the tray before the naturally slow seed emerges.

crops.name: Lemongrass
crops.summary: A durable tropical herb that forms a large fragrant clump.
crops.watering: Water deeply, allowing good drainage.
instructions: Begin with a rooted division for reliable growth.
Plant in a roomy container.
Cut mature outer stalks close to the base.
crops.common_mistake: Using a container too small for the expanding clump.

crops.name: Curry Leaf
crops.summary: A long-lived tropical aromatic shrub for a large sunny container.
crops.watering: Water deeply, then allow the surface to dry.
instructions: Choose a healthy nursery plant for faster results.
Repot gradually as roots fill the container.
Pinch growing tips to encourage branching.
crops.common_mistake: Keeping roots constantly wet during cooler periods.

crops.name: Marigold
crops.summary: A cheerful, dependable flower for sunny containers and borders.
crops.watering: Water at the base when the surface dries.
instructions: Sow in a tray or small nursery pot.
Transplant after several true leaves form.
Remove faded flowers to continue blooming.
crops.common_mistake: Overwatering or leaving faded blooms on the plant.

crops.name: Sunflower
crops.summary: A bold fast-growing annual for full sun and deeper containers.
crops.watering: Water deeply after the surface begins to dry.
instructions: Sow directly in the final position.
Retain one sturdy seedling per pot.
Stake tall varieties before flower heads become heavy.
crops.common_mistake: Moving established seedlings or using a shallow pot.

crops.name: Zinnia
crops.summary: A colourful warm-season annual that flowers quickly in full sun.
crops.watering: Water at soil level and let foliage stay dry.
instructions: Sow directly into warm soil.
Thin without disturbing retained seedlings.
Deadhead regularly for repeat flowers.
crops.common_mistake: Overhead watering and crowding, which encourages leaf disease.

crops.name: Cosmos
crops.summary: An airy flowering annual that thrives in sun and moderately lean soil.
crops.watering: Water moderately and avoid constantly rich, wet soil.
instructions: Sow directly and cover lightly.
Thin to prevent lanky growth.
Pinch once, then deadhead through the season.
crops.common_mistake: Overfeeding, which produces leaves instead of flowers.

crops.name: Balsam
crops.summary: A colourful monsoon-friendly annual for moist but draining soil.
crops.watering: Keep evenly moist, particularly in warm breezy weather.
instructions: Sow after severe heat begins to ease.
Thin crowded seedlings.
Pinch tips once to encourage branches.
crops.common_mistake: Allowing seedlings to remain crowded and weak.

crops.name: Petunia
crops.summary: A cool-season container flower with a long display in gentle sun.
crops.watering: Water when the surface dries and ensure fast drainage.
instructions: Press tiny seed onto the surface without burying it.
Transplant carefully after true leaves develop.
Remove faded flowers and pinch trailing stems.
crops.common_mistake: Covering the tiny light-sensitive seed too deeply.

crops.name: Dianthus
crops.summary: A compact cool-season flower for pots, edges and sunny balconies.
crops.watering: Water moderately and keep the crown dry.
instructions: Sow thinly in a cool nursery tray.
Transplant into gritty, draining soil.
Deadhead stems back to leafy growth.
crops.common_mistake: Keeping the crown constantly wet.

crops.name: Calendula
crops.summary: An easy cool-season flower with bright edible-looking petals and sturdy growth.
crops.watering: Water when the surface starts to dry.
instructions: Sow directly in cool, draining soil.
Thin to prevent crowding.
Deadhead flowers before seed forms.
crops.common_mistake: Starting too late as hot weather arrives.

crops.name: Portulaca
crops.summary: A low, heat-loving succulent annual for hot sunny containers.
crops.watering: Water sparingly after the plant establishes.
instructions: Press tiny seed onto a gritty moist surface.
Move seedlings to the sun gradually.
Allow soil to dry slightly between waterings.
crops.common_mistake: Overwatering this drought-tolerant plant.

crops.name: Nasturtium
crops.summary: A trailing cool-season flower for rail planters, pots and garden edges.
crops.watering: Water moderately; avoid overly rich, wet soil.
instructions: Soak the large seed for a few hours.
Sow directly where the plant will trail.
Use light support for climbing forms.
crops.common_mistake: Overfeeding, which creates leaves with few flowers.
